text.title: Rust Testing Anti-Patterns and Agent Gotchas Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real rust-testing-antipatterns deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tests sprinkled with tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)); flake on slow CI runners.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used real sleep instead of virtual time.

antipattern.symptom: assert_eq!(record.created_at, Utc::now()); flake on every run.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed clock would tick zero between record write and assertion.
antipattern.fix: Inject a Clock trait or compare with tolerance; never assert against the wall clock.

antipattern.symptom: static mut COUNTER: u64 = 0; tests fail in parallel mode.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated tests like a single-threaded mainline.
antipattern.fix: Per-test fixtures via #[fixture] / once_cell::sync::Lazy with isolation.

antipattern.symptom: Tests bind to 127.0.0.1:8080; parallel runs collide; 'address in use'.
antipattern.fix: Bind to port 0; read assigned port from the listener.

antipattern.root-cause: Agent fabricated mockall predicate that does not exist in the API.
antipattern.fix: Cross-reference mockall docs; CI runs cargo check before review.
